mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates reduce corporate profit margins and dampen risk appetite.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes in >65% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings revisions and recession fears. This threshold breach triggers immediate sell-offs in equities over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength. This joint demand surge drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization. This supply-side rebound fuels short-term equity rallies over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in >65% of recovery-phase months with rising INDPRO, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index level exceeds 300.0, it signals entrenched inflation that erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank tightening. This threshold breach suppresses forward equity returns over the next month as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion. This dual labor strength supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts negative returns in >60% of months, or the joint labor metric shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in housing starts reflects strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity. This demand indicator leads broader economic expansion, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter as capital expenditure increases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with rising HOUST, or the trend signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising federal funds rates during a late-cycle expansion signals a supply-demand mismatch and policy-induced slowdown. This combination triggers equity sell-offs over the next month as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >70% of late-cycle months with falling INDPRO and rising FEDFUNDS, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy.
